summary: Surface stage complete. Mapped live attack surface of OWASP Juice Shop v20.1.1 at http://127.0.0.1:3010. Confirmed multiple live surfaces including SQL injection in login and product search, excessive data exposure via API, directory listing enabled, sensitive files exposed, JWT with embedded password hash, and various API endpoints.

surfaces.description: SQL injection in email field - authenticated as admin using payload ' OR 1=1-- as email. Response returned JWT token with admin privileges.
surfaces.evidence: POST /rest/user/login with body {"email":"' OR 1=1--","password":"test"} returned 200 with token containing role:admin, email:admin@juice-sh.op

surfaces.description: SQL injection in search parameter q - UNION SELECT injection confirmed. Error 'SQLITE_ERROR: incomplete input' on single quote, successful UNION SELECT returning injected values.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/products/search?q=')) UNION SELECT 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 FROM sqlite_master-- returned injected row with id:1, name:2, description:3, price:4

surfaces.description: Excessive data exposure - authenticated GET returns all users including password hashes, roles, email addresses. No access control filtering.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Users with admin JWT returned 25 users with fields: id, email, password (hash), role, profileImage, isActive

surfaces.description: Product API returns full product catalog with pricing, descriptions, images. No auth required.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Products returned all products including deleted items with deletedAt timestamps

surfaces.description: Feedback API returns all user feedback comments with UserId mapping. No auth required.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Feedbacks returned 8 feedbacks including user comments with email placeholders and UserId

surfaces.description: Challenges API exposes all challenge definitions including descriptions, difficulty ratings, and solve status. No auth required.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Challenges returned full challenge list including solved status (e.g., 'Admin Registration' already solved)

surfaces.description: Inventory quantity API exposes stock levels and per-user limits. No auth required.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Quantitys returned 56 inventory records with quantity and limitPerUser fields

surfaces.description: Security questions API exposes all password reset questions. No auth required.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/SecurityQuestions returned 14 security questions

surfaces.description: Application configuration leak - exposes OAuth client ID, challenge configs, XSS bonus payload, product details, social links, and more.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/admin/application-configuration returned config including googleOauth clientId, xssBonusPayload, challenge settings

surfaces.description: Application version disclosure.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/admin/application-version returned {"version":"20.1.1"}

surfaces.description: Captcha endpoint reveals captcha answers in plain text, defeating the purpose of captcha.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/captcha/ returned {"captchaId":0,"captcha":"9-7-3","answer":"-1"}

surfaces.description: Continue code endpoint exposes the game's continue code used for progress recovery.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/continue-code returned continueCode value

surfaces.description: Basket API with auth returns basket contents including products and quantities. Potential IDOR if basket ID enumeration possible.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/basket/1 with admin JWT returned basket with Product items and BasketItem details

surfaces.description: Password change endpoint works with GET request using query parameters. Changes password when valid current password provided.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/user/change-password?current=admin123&new=newpass123&repeat=newpass123 with auth returned updated user with new password hash

surfaces.description: Complaints endpoint exists but requires authentication (returns 401 without auth).
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Complaints returned 401 'No Authorization header was found'

surfaces.description: Cards endpoint exists but returns 401 with 'Cannot read properties of undefined' error - likely requires auth but also has internal error.
surfaces.evidence: GET /api/Cards returned 401 with error message about undefined data

surfaces.description: Directory listing enabled on /ftp/ - exposes sensitive files including confidential documents, database backups, and internal files.
surfaces.evidence: GET /ftp/ returned directory listing with files: acquisitions.md (confidential), incident-support.kdbx (KeePass), suspicious_errors.yml, package.json.bak, coupons_2013.md.bak, etc.

surfaces.description: Confidential document 'Planned Acquisitions' accessible without authentication. Contains sensitive business information.
surfaces.evidence: GET /ftp/acquisitions.md returned document marked 'This document is confidential! Do not distribute!'

surfaces.description: Legal information file accessible without auth. Serves as static file from /ftp/ directory.
surfaces.evidence: GET /ftp/legal.md returned markdown content

surfaces.description: Subdirectory under /ftp/ with directory listing enabled.
surfaces.evidence: GET /ftp/quarantine/ returned directory listing

surfaces.description: Whoami endpoint returns empty user object with valid JWT. May not properly identify the authenticated user.
surfaces.evidence: GET /rest/user/whoami with admin JWT returned {"user":{}}

surfaces.description: Password reset endpoint has rate limiting (x-ratelimit-limit: 100) and validates against security questions.
surfaces.evidence: POST /rest/user/reset-password with wrong answer returned 401 'Wrong answer to security question' with rate limit headers

surfaces.description: NoSQL injection vector identified - passing JSON object as password causes Type Error when trying to hash an Object, indicating the app doesn't properly validate input types.
surfaces.evidence: POST /rest/user/login with {"email":"admin@juice-sh.op","password":{"$ne":""}} returned 500 'The "data" argument must be of type string or an instance of Buffer'

surfaces.description: JWT tokens embed user password hashes in the payload. Token uses RS256 algorithm. Payload contains id, email, password hash, role, and other user data.
surfaces.evidence: Decoded JWT payload: {"data":{"id":1,"email":"admin@juice-sh.op","password":"0192023a7bbd73250516f069df18b500"...},"bid":1,"iat":1784837696}

surfaces.description: B2B order endpoint exists - potentially vulnerable to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) via customerReference field.
surfaces.evidence: POST /b2b/v2/order with auth returned SPA index.html (handled by Angular router) - endpoint requires further SSTI probing

candidates.claim: SQL injection in login form (email/password fields) - CONFIRMED via ' OR 1=1-- payload
candidates.evidence: Authenticated as admin with SQL injection

candidates.claim: SQL injection in product search parameter - CONFIRMED via UNION SELECT
candidates.evidence: UNION SELECT returned injected data in product results

candidates.claim: SQL injection in basket item operations - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: Basket operations may be injectable

candidates.claim: NoSQL injection in registration/login endpoint - PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (password field accepts objects causing type error)
candidates.evidence: Server crashes when password is JSON object instead of string

candidates.claim: Reflected XSS in search results - NEGATIVE (search returns JSON, no HTML reflection)
candidates.evidence: XSS payload in q parameter returned empty data array, not reflected

candidates.claim: Stored XSS in feedback submission - UNTESTED (captcha blocks without captchaId)
candidates.evidence: Feedback POST requires captchaId parameter

candidates.claim: DOM-based XSS via hash fragment - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: Angular SPA may have DOM XSS vectors

candidates.claim: IDOR on user profiles - CONFIRMED (admin can access all user data via /api/Users)
candidates.evidence: Authenticated GET /api/Users returns all users

candidates.claim: IDOR on basket access - CONFIRMED (admin can access basket/1)
candidates.evidence: GET /rest/basket/1 with admin JWT returned basket contents

candidates.claim: IDOR on order history - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: Order endpoints need authentication testing

candidates.claim: JWT token weaknesses - CONFIRMED (password hash embedded in token)
candidates.evidence: JWT payload contains password hash in plaintext

candidates.claim: Login brute force / rate limiting test - PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (rate limiting present on reset-password)
candidates.evidence: Reset password has x-ratelimit-limit: 100 headers

candidates.claim: Password reset flaws - PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (security question validation present)
candidates.evidence: POST to reset-password validates answers

candidates.claim: Server-Side Template Injection in B2B order API - UNTESTED (requires authenticated probe)
candidates.evidence: B2B order endpoint exists at /b2b/v2/order

candidates.claim: Path traversal in static file serving - PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (/ftp/ accessible, but ../ normalized to index.html)
candidates.evidence: Directory traversal via /ftp/../package.json returns SPA index.html

candidates.claim: Path traversal via FTP endpoint - CONFIRMED (directory listing enabled, files accessible)
candidates.evidence: /ftp/ directory listing reveals sensitive files

candidates.claim: Malicious file upload (profile picture) - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: Profile image update endpoint not fully tested

candidates.claim: CSRF on state-changing endpoints - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: No CSRF token validation observed

candidates.claim: SSRF via product review image URL - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: Product review/image endpoints not fully tested

candidates.claim: Cross-Site Script Inclusion - UNTESTED
candidates.evidence: JSON responses have proper content-type headers

facts.summary: OWASP Juice Shop v20.1.1 running Express ^4.22.1 on port 3010
facts.body: Target identified as OWASP Juice Shop version 20.1.1. Application is a Node.js/Express web app with Angular SPA frontend. Uses SQLite database (Sequelize ORM). JWT-based authentication with RS256 algorithm.

facts.summary: JWT-based authentication with embedded user data
facts.body: Login endpoint /rest/user/login returns JWT token containing full user object including password hash. Token uses RS256 (RSA with SHA-256). Admin user has id=1, email=admin@juice-sh.op, role=admin. Password hash '0192023a7bbd73250516f069df18b500' is MD5 of 'admin123'.

facts.summary: Obtained admin JWT token via SQL injection
facts.body: Using SQL injection payload ' OR 1=1-- as email with any password, logged in as admin (id=1). Retrieved admin JWT token allowing access to /api/Users, /rest/basket/1, and other privileged endpoints.

Files accessible: acquisitions.md (confidential), announcement_encrypted.md, coupons_2013.md.bak, eastere.gg, encrypt.pyc, incident-support.kdbx (KeePass database), legal.md, order PDFs, package-lock.json.bak, package.json.bak, suspicious_errors.yml, quarantine/ subdirectory.

deadends.reason: Path traversal via ../ is neutralized - returns SPA index.html instead of target file
File extension blocked - only .md and .pdf allowed by fileServer route
Endpoint returns Angular SPA instead of API response (SPA route handler catches it)
Returns Angular SPA - likely handled by SPA route handler
Returns Angular SPA - likely handled by SPA route handler
Returns Angular SPA - likely handled by SPA route handler
XSS payload in search query returns empty data, no reflection
